tasks.subject: [agents-page] [P1] Create AgentRow expandable list row component
tasks.description:
Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/ui/AgentRow.tsx` — a dense expandable agent row component using the `Collapsible` primitive from shared/ui. ## Component Interface ```tsx interface AgentRowProps { agent: AgentManifest; sessionCount: number; healthStatus: 'active' | 'inactive' | 'stale'; lastActive: string | null; } ``` Import `AgentManifest` from `@dorkos/shared/mesh-schemas`. ## Collapsed State (~56px height) Render a horizontal row with these elements left-to-right: 1. **Health/status dot**: `` with color classes: - `'active'` → `bg-emerald-500` - `'inactive'` → `bg-amber-500` - `'stale'` → `bg-muted-foreground/30` 2. **Agent name**: `{agent.name}` 3. **Runtime badge**: `{agent.runtime}` — import Badge from `@/layers/shared/ui/badge` 4. **Project path**: `{truncatedPath}` — show last 2 path segments (e.g., `/home/user/projects/frontend` → `projects/frontend`). Use a helper: `function truncatePath(fullPath: string): string { const segments = fullPath.split('/').filter(Boolean); return segments.length <= 2 ? fullPath : segments.slice(-2).join('/'); }` 5. **Active session count**: `{sessionCount} active` — only render when `sessionCount > 0` 6. **Capability badges**: Show max 3 capabilities as `{cap}`. If `agent.capabilities.length > 3`, show a `"+{N} more"` overflow badge after the first 3. 7. **Last active**: `{lastActive}` — render relative timestamp string passed as prop (e.g., `"2m ago"`, `"1h ago"`, `"Mar 15"`). If `lastActive` is null, render nothing. 8. **Action button**: Render `` (imported from sibling file). 9. **Chevron**: `` icon from lucide-react. Apply `transition-transform duration-200` and `rotate-180` when expanded. Use `Collapsible` and `CollapsibleTrigger` from `@/layers/shared/ui/collapsible` for the expand/collapse behavior. The entire collapsed row is the trigger area (except the action button). ## Expanded State (~120px additional height) Render inside `CollapsibleContent`: - Full description: `

{agent.description}

` - All capabilities: full badge list (no truncation) - Behavior config: response mode, escalation threshold (from `agent.behavior` if present) - Budget limits: max hops, max calls per hour (from `agent.budget` if present) - Registration date + registeredBy (from `agent.registeredAt`, `agent.registeredBy` if present) - Namespace: `Namespace: {agent.namespace}` (if set) - Edit button: `` — opens `AgentDialog` from `@/layers/features/agent-settings` with the agent's `projectPath` - Unregister button: `` — on click, show an inline confirmation ("Are you sure?") before calling `useUnregisterAgent().mutate(agent.id)` from `@/layers/entities/mesh` The expanded section uses `
` for layout. ## Styling Row container: `
`. Collapsed content uses `flex items-center gap-3`. ## Test File Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/__tests__/AgentRow.test.tsx`: ```tsx /** * @vitest-environment jsdom */ import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest'; import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react'; import '@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest'; import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from '@tanstack/react-query'; ``` Mock dependencies: - `vi.mock('@/layers/entities/mesh', ...)` — mock `useUnregisterAgent` returning `{ mutate: vi.fn() }` - `vi.mock('@/layers/entities/session', ...)` — mock `useSessions` returning `{ data: [], isLoading: false }` - `vi.mock('@tanstack/react-router', ...)` — mock `useNavigate` returning `vi.fn()` - Mock `SessionLaunchPopover` as a simple button to isolate AgentRow tests - Mock `AgentDialog` as a no-op component Test cases: 1. `it('renders collapsed row with name, runtime badge, and truncated path')` — verify agent name, runtime badge text, and truncated path are visible 2. `it('shows health status dot with correct color class')` — check for the dot element with appropriate class for each status 3. `it('expands on chevron click revealing full details')` — click the row, verify description and all capabilities appear 4. `it('shows Start Session when no active sessions')` — sessionCount=0, verify button text 5. `it('shows Open Session with badge when sessions exist')` — sessionCount=2, verify badge 6. `it('truncates capabilities at 3 with +N more badge')` — pass agent with 5 capabilities, verify only 3 shown + "+2 more" 7. `it('calls unregister mutation on unregister confirm')` — expand, click unregister, confirm, verify mutate called Wrap renders in `QueryClientProvider`. ## Acceptance Criteria - Collapsed row displays all 9 elements described above - Expanding reveals full agent details - Chevron rotates on expand/collapse - Capability overflow shows "+N more" badge - Unregister requires confirmation before mutation - All 7 test cases pass
tasks.activeForm: Creating the AgentRow expandable list row component

tasks.subject: [agents-page] [P1] Create SessionLaunchPopover for agent session management
tasks.description:
Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/ui/SessionLaunchPopover.tsx` — a component that handles session launch from an agent row, with two behavioral modes. ## Component Interface ```tsx interface SessionLaunchPopoverProps { agent: AgentManifest; } ``` Import `AgentManifest` from `@dorkos/shared/mesh-schemas`. ## Dependencies - `useSessions()` from `@/layers/entities/session` — returns `{ data: Session[], isLoading: boolean }` - `useNavigate()` from `@tanstack/react-router` - `Popover`, `PopoverTrigger`, `PopoverContent` from `@/layers/shared/ui/popover` - `Button` from `@/layers/shared/ui/button` ## Session Matching Logic Filter sessions that match the agent's project path: ```tsx const { data: allSessions = [] } = useSessions(); const agentSessions = useMemo( () => allSessions.filter((s) => s.cwd === agent.projectPath || s.dir === agent.projectPath), [allSessions, agent.projectPath] ); const activeCount = agentSessions.length; ``` Note: Check the actual session schema fields — the session data may use `cwd`, `dir`, or `projectPath`. Inspect `useSessions` return type and use the matching field. If no field matches `agent.projectPath`, fall back to path-based comparison (check if session cwd starts with or equals agent project path). ## Mode 1: No Active Sessions (activeCount === 0) Render a plain button (no popover): ```tsx ``` ## Mode 2: Has Active Sessions (activeCount > 0) Render a `Popover`: ```tsx
{agentSessions.map((session) => ( ))}
``` ## Test File Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/__tests__/SessionLaunchPopover.test.tsx`: Mock dependencies: - `vi.mock('@tanstack/react-router', () => ({ useNavigate: () => mockNavigate }))` - `vi.mock('@/layers/entities/session', () => ({ useSessions: () => mockUseSessions() }))` Test agent fixture: ```tsx const agent = { id: 'agent-1', name: 'TestAgent', runtime: 'claude-code', projectPath: '/home/user/projects/frontend', capabilities: [], namespace: 'default', }; ``` Test cases: 1. `it('renders Start Session button when no active sessions')` — mock useSessions returning empty, verify "Start Session" text 2. `it('navigates to /session with dir param on Start Session click')` — click button, verify `mockNavigate` called with `{ to: '/session', search: { dir: '/home/user/projects/frontend' } }` 3. `it('renders Open Session button with badge when sessions exist')` — mock 2 sessions, verify "Open Session" text and badge with "2" 4. `it('shows popover with session list on Open Session click')` — click button, verify session IDs visible in popover 5. `it('navigates to /session with session param on Resume click')` — click a session row, verify navigate called with session ID 6. `it('navigates to /session with dir param on New Session click')` — click "New Session" in popover, verify navigate with dir ## Acceptance Criteria - No-session mode renders plain button with direct navigation - Has-session mode renders popover with session list - Each session row shows truncated ID and last message preview - "New Session" button at bottom of popover - All 6 test cases pass

tasks.subject: [agents-page] [P1] Create agents-list barrel export
tasks.description:
Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/index.ts` — the barrel export for the agents-list feature module.

```tsx
/**
 * Agent list feature — dense fleet management list with filtering, expandable rows, and session launch.
 *
 * @module features/agents-list
 */
export { AgentRow } from './ui/AgentRow';
export { AgentFilterBar } from './ui/AgentFilterBar';
export { AgentsList } from './ui/AgentsList';
export { SessionLaunchPopover } from './ui/SessionLaunchPopover';
```

This follows the FSD barrel export convention documented in `contributing/project-structure.md`. Every FSD module must have an `index.ts` with a module-level TSDoc comment describing purpose and layer role.

## Acceptance Criteria

- File exports all 4 components
- Module-level TSDoc comment present
- Imports from the barrel work: `import { AgentsList } from '@/layers/features/agents-list'`

tasks.subject: [agents-page] [P2] Create AgentsHeader and update top-nav barrel
tasks.description:
Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/top-nav/ui/AgentsHeader.tsx` — the page header rendered in the AppShell header slot for the `/agents` route. Also update the top-nav barrel export to include AgentsHeader. ## AgentsHeader Implementation ```tsx import { useState } from 'react'; import { ScanSearch } from 'lucide-react'; import { Button } from '@/layers/shared/ui/button'; import { ResponsiveDialog, ResponsiveDialogContent, ResponsiveDialogHeader, ResponsiveDialogTitle, } from '@/layers/shared/ui/responsive-dialog'; import { CommandPaletteTrigger } from './CommandPaletteTrigger'; import { DiscoveryView } from '@/layers/features/mesh'; /** Page header for the /agents route — title, scan trigger, and command palette. */ export function AgentsHeader() { const [discoveryOpen, setDiscoveryOpen] = useState(false); return ( <> Agents
Discover Agents ); } ``` Note: Check the exact ResponsiveDialog API — the spec shows a simpler `` pattern, but the actual implementation may need `ResponsiveDialogContent` wrapper. Look at existing usage in the codebase (e.g., OverlayShowcases) for the correct composition pattern. Note on DiscoveryView import: Same as AgentsPage — ensure `DiscoveryView` is exported from `features/mesh/index.ts`. ## Update top-nav barrel Modify `apps/client/src/layers/features/top-nav/index.ts` to add: ```tsx export { AgentsHeader } from './ui/AgentsHeader'; ``` Add it after the existing `DashboardHeader` export. ## Create widgets/agents barrel Create `apps/client/src/layers/widgets/agents/index.ts`: ```tsx /** * Agents widget — dedicated fleet management page at /agents. * * @module widgets/agents */ export { AgentsPage } from './ui/AgentsPage'; ``` ## Ensure DiscoveryView is exported from mesh barrel Check `apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/index.ts`. If `DiscoveryView` is not already exported, add: ```tsx export { DiscoveryView } from './ui/DiscoveryView'; ``` ## Test File Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/top-nav/__tests__/AgentsHeader.test.tsx`: Mock dependencies: - `vi.mock('@/layers/features/mesh', ...)` — mock `DiscoveryView` as a div with testid - `vi.mock('./CommandPaletteTrigger', ...)` — mock as a button with testid - Mock ResponsiveDialog components to render children directly Test cases: 1. `it('renders page title Agents')` — verify "Agents" text present 2. `it('renders Scan for Agents button')` — verify button with text 3. `it('opens discovery dialog on Scan button click')` — click button, verify DiscoveryView testid appears 4. `it('renders CommandPaletteTrigger')` — verify command palette trigger testid ## Acceptance Criteria - Header shows "Agents" title on the left - "Scan for Agents" button with ScanSearch icon - Clicking scan opens ResponsiveDialog with DiscoveryView - CommandPaletteTrigger on the right - top-nav barrel updated with AgentsHeader export - widgets/agents barrel created - DiscoveryView exported from mesh barrel (if not already) - All 4 test cases pass

tasks.subject: [agents-page] [P3] Remove Agents tab from MeshPanel
[agents-page] [P4] Add /agents route to router.tsx
tasks.description:
Add the `/agents` route to `apps/client/src/router.tsx`. This route uses the `appShellRoute` as parent and lazy-loads the `AgentsPage` widget.

## Changes to router.tsx

Add the agentsRoute definition after the sessionRoute:

```tsx
// ── Agents at /agents ───────────────────────────────────────
const agentsRoute = createRoute({
  getParentRoute: () => appShellRoute,
  path: '/agents',
  component: lazy(() =>
    import('@/layers/widgets/agents').then((m) => ({ default: m.AgentsPage }))
  ),
});
```

Note: Use `lazy()` from React for route-level code splitting since most users won't visit `/agents` on every session. Import `lazy` at the top: add it to the existing React import or add `import { lazy } from 'react';`.

Alternatively, if TanStack Router supports `lazyRouteComponent` or a similar API, check the existing patterns (the current `indexRoute` and `sessionRoute` use static `component` imports — so either approach works). Lazy is preferred per the spec to reduce initial bundle.

Update the route tree to include agentsRoute:

```tsx
// ── Route tree ──────────────────────────────────────────────
const routeTree = rootRoute.addChildren([
  appShellRoute.addChildren([indexRoute, sessionRoute, agentsRoute]),
]);
```

The full diff for router.tsx:

1. Add `import { lazy } from 'react';` (if not already imported)
2. Add the `agentsRoute` const after `sessionRoute`
3. Update the `routeTree` to include `agentsRoute` in the appShellRoute children array

## Acceptance Criteria

- `/agents` route is defined under appShellRoute
- AgentsPage lazy-loads when navigating to /agents
- Route tree includes agentsRoute
- TypeScript compiles without errors
- Existing routes (/, /session) continue to work

tasks.subject: [agents-page] [P4] Add Agents nav item to DashboardSidebar and update tests
tasks.description:
Update `apps/client/src/layers/features/dashboard-sidebar/ui/DashboardSidebar.tsx` to add "Agents" as a third navigation item. Also update its test file.

## Changes to DashboardSidebar.tsx

Add the `Users` icon import from lucide-react and `useRouterState` from TanStack Router:

```tsx
import { LayoutDashboard, MessageSquare, Users } from 'lucide-react';
import { useNavigate, useRouterState } from '@tanstack/react-router';
```

Inside the component, read the current pathname:
```tsx
const pathname = useRouterState({ select: (s) => s.location.pathname });
```

Update the `isActive` prop on the Dashboard button to be pathname-aware:
```tsx
<SidebarMenuButton
  isActive={pathname === '/'}
  onClick={() => navigate({ to: '/' })}
  className="text-foreground flex w-full items-center gap-1.5 rounded-md px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs font-medium"
>
  <LayoutDashboard className="size-(--size-icon-sm)" />
  Dashboard
</SidebarMenuButton>
```

Update the Sessions button to also be pathname-aware:
```tsx
<SidebarMenuButton
  isActive={pathname === '/session'}
  onClick={() => navigate({ to: '/session' })}
  className="..." // same existing classes
>
  <MessageSquare className="size-(--size-icon-sm)" />
  Sessions
</SidebarMenuButton>
```

Add the Agents nav item after Sessions:
```tsx
<SidebarMenuItem>
  <SidebarMenuButton
    isActive={pathname === '/agents'}
    onClick={() => navigate({ to: '/agents' })}
    className="text-muted-foreground hover:bg-accent hover:text-foreground flex w-full items-center gap-1.5 rounded-md px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs font-medium transition-all duration-100 active:scale-[0.98]"
  >
    <Users className="size-(--size-icon-sm)" />
    Agents
  </SidebarMenuButton>
</SidebarMenuItem>
```

Note: The active state styling is handled by `SidebarMenuButton`'s `isActive` prop which applies the active variant styling. The className should use `text-muted-foreground` for the base state (same as the Sessions button). When `isActive` is true, the SidebarMenuButton component internally applies `text-foreground` styling.

Also update the placeholder content — remove or replace the `"Agent overview coming soon"` text since agents now have their own page:
```tsx
<SidebarContent className="flex flex-1 items-center justify-center p-6">
  {/* Content area — available for future dashboard sidebar widgets */}
</SidebarContent>
```

Or remove the SidebarContent section entirely if it's just a placeholder.

## Update DashboardSidebar Tests

Modify `apps/client/src/layers/features/dashboard-sidebar/__tests__/DashboardSidebar.test.tsx`:

Update the router mock to include `useRouterState`:
```tsx
const mockNavigate = vi.fn();
vi.mock('@tanstack/react-router', () => ({
  useNavigate: () => mockNavigate,
  useRouterState: ({ select }: { select: (s: { location: { pathname: string } }) => string }) =>
    select({ location: { pathname: '/' } }),
}));
```

Add test cases:

1. `it('renders Agents nav item')` — verify "Agents" text appears at least once (SidebarProvider renders desktop + mobile)
   ```tsx
   it('renders Agents nav item', () => {
     renderWithProviders(<DashboardSidebar />);
     const items = screen.getAllByText('Agents');
     expect(items.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
   });
   ```

2. `it('navigates to /agents on Agents click')` — click the Agents button, verify navigate called
   ```tsx
   it('navigates to /agents on Agents click', () => {
     renderWithProviders(<DashboardSidebar />);
     const agentsButtons = screen.getAllByText('Agents');
     fireEvent.click(agentsButtons[0]);
     expect(mockNavigate).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ to: '/agents' });
   });
   ```

3. Update the placeholder content test — if "Agent overview coming soon" text is removed, update or remove the test that checks for it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- DashboardSidebar shows 3 nav items: Dashboard, Sessions, Agents
- Each nav item has correct `isActive` state based on pathname
- Clicking Agents navigates to `/agents`
- Placeholder text removed or updated
- All DashboardSidebar tests pass including new ones
- No regression in existing Dashboard and Sessions navigation
tasks.activeForm: Adding Agents nav item to DashboardSidebar and updating tests

tasks.subject: [agents-page] [P4] Update AGENTS.md routing documentation
tasks.description:
Update the Routing section in `AGENTS.md` to include the new `/agents` route.

In the **Routing** subsection under **Client**, update the route structure:

Change:
```
- `/` -> `DashboardPage` (widgets/dashboard) -- status overview, with `DashboardSidebar` + `DashboardHeader`
- `/session` -> `SessionPage` (widgets/session) -- agent chat, with `SessionSidebar` + `SessionHeader`, `?session=` and `?dir=` search params
```

To:
```
- `/` -> `DashboardPage` (widgets/dashboard) -- status overview, with `DashboardSidebar` + `DashboardHeader`
- `/agents` -> `AgentsPage` (widgets/agents) -- fleet management, with `DashboardSidebar` + `AgentsHeader`
- `/session` -> `SessionPage` (widgets/session) -- agent chat, with `SessionSidebar` + `SessionHeader`, `?session=` and `?dir=` search params
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- AGENTS.md routing section lists `/agents` route
- Route description matches actual implementation
- Sidebar and header components correctly documented
